title: Lesson 20: Large Language Models Part 20 — Capstone Project: Cricket Commentary Generation System

sections.content: Project Overview

This capstone project challenges you to build a Cricket Commentary Generation System powered by a fine-tuned Large Language Model. The system ingests live match data (runs scored, wickets fallen, ball-by-ball sequences, player statistics) and generates authentic, contextually-aware cricket commentary in real time. The LLM learns patterns from historical cricket commentary corpora and produces output that captures the energy, terminology, and narrative arc of professional broadcasting. This project integrates multiple LLM techniques: prompt engineering for match context injection, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to ground commentary in live stats, fine-tuning on cricket-specific corpora, and token streaming for real-time generation. The portfolio value is substantial: you demonstrate end-to-end LLM engineering, domain adaptation, production API design, and the ability to build consumer-facing AI products. Companies building sports analytics platforms, streaming services, and fantasy cricket apps actively hire engineers with this skill set. This project also surfaces the critical challenge of hallucination mitigation—the model must remain factually consistent with match data while generating creative, engaging narrative.

🏏 Think of it like cricket: A live cricket match unfolds ball-by-ball with raw data: batsman scores a 4, fast bowler takes a wicket on a yorker delivery, run rate climbs, fielding captain adjusts the field. A professional commentator doesn't just read these facts—they weave them into a narrative that captures momentum, builds tension, and contextualizes every event within the match situation and player history. Your LLM becomes that commentator. The match data (runs, wickets, economy rate) flows in as context, the model's training on thousands of past commentaries provides the 'voice and style', and the generated output is the live broadcast commentary. Just as a great commentator like Harsha Bhogle instantly connects a boundary hit to the batsman's form, pitch conditions, and match situation, your LLM must combine live stats with contextual understanding to produce output that feels real, informed, and timely. The challenge mirrors what commentators face: generating fresh, engaging narrative under time pressure without fabricating facts. Understanding this parallel reveals why prompt engineering matters—how you frame the match context directly shapes whether the commentary is insightful or hallucinated.
Learning Objectives

items: Master fine-tuning LLMs on domain-specific corpora (cricket commentary datasets) using adapter layers, LoRA, or full parameter updates.
Implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines that ground model outputs in live match statistics, preventing hallucination.
Design effective prompt templates that inject structured match context (batting avg, strike rate, economy, pitch info) into generation.
Build streaming APIs that produce real-time token outputs, enabling live commentary generation with sub-second latency.
Apply quantization, batching, and inference optimization techniques to deploy large models within resource constraints.
Implement evaluation metrics (factual consistency scoring, BLEU, sentiment alignment) to measure commentary quality beyond generic perplexity.

sections.content: Technical Requirements

items: Ingest live match data via simulated WebSocket stream or batch API, including ball-by-ball logs, player stats, and innings context.
Fine-tune a base LLM (e.g., GPT-2, Llama-2, or OpenAI API) on a cricket commentary corpus (collect from ESPN Cricinfo, YouTube transcripts).
Implement vector-based retrieval system (FAISS, Pinecone, or Weaviate) storing historical match summaries, player profiles, and commentary examples.
Design prompt template that dynamically constructs context: current match state, recent deliveries, player streaks, historical head-to-head stats.
Build token-streaming endpoint that yields commentary text incrementally, simulating live broadcast feed with configurable generation parameters.
Apply guardrails: fact-checker that validates commentary against match data, rejects hallucinated claims about scores or wickets.
Deploy model with quantization (8-bit, 4-bit) to reduce memory footprint, enabling deployment on standard cloud instances.
Implement observability: log token counts, latency, fact-check failures, and user feedback for continuous model improvement.

sections.content: Architecture & Design

The system follows a modular pipeline architecture: (1) Match Data Ingestion Layer—consumes live ball-by-ball data, updates player statistics, maintains innings context via a time-series database or in-memory store. (2) Context Enrichment Module—queries the vector retrieval system to fetch relevant historical matches, player records, and similar commentary examples; constructs a structured prompt combining current match state with retrieved context. (3) LLM Generation Engine—accepts the enriched prompt, streams tokens from a fine-tuned language model with temperature and top-k sampling configured for coherent but creative output. (4) Fact-Checking & Grounding Layer—validates each generated sentence against live match data, blocks claims that contradict scorecard (e.g., 'he scored 50 runs' when he actually scored 30), and flags uncertain claims. (5) API & Streaming Interface—exposes HTTP endpoints for commentary requests and WebSocket connections for real-time token streaming to clients. Key design decisions: asynchronous token generation to avoid blocking; caching of context embeddings and match summaries to reduce per-commentary latency; separation of generation and fact-checking to allow parallel processing; configuration-driven prompts to enable A/B testing of commentary styles. The architecture scales horizontally: multiple inference replicas behind a load balancer handle concurrent match feeds, vector retrieval is distributed across shards, and the fact-checker runs as a separate microservice.

🏏 Think of it like cricket: A cricket match broadcast involves multiple coordinated teams working in parallel. The analyst room collects live match data—runs, wickets, pitch behavior—and feeds it to the commentary box. The commentators (your LLM generation engine) draw on their years of experience (fine-tuned knowledge) and the analyst team (retrieval system) continuously whispers relevant historical context: 'Bumrah's economy in powerplays is 4.2', 'Sharma has a weakness against left-arm spinners'. As the commentator speaks, a fact-checker (likely the producer off-mic) ensures no false claims are broadcast—if a commentator says 'first century on this ground,' the producer verifies it before it goes to air, or it gets corrected immediately. The broadcast director (API layer) manages timing, ensuring commentary flows in real time without delays. Just as a coordinated broadcast team produces fluid, factually accurate, contextually aware commentary, your architecture coordinates data ingestion, context retrieval, generation, and fact-checking to produce reliable, engaging output. This reveals why decoupled microservices matter: separation of concerns mirrors the broadcast workflow, and parallel processing ensures latency doesn't degrade user experience.

# Project Structure & Architecture Skeleton

project_structure = """
cricket_commentary_system/
├── data/
│   ├── commentary_corpus/          # Historical commentary transcripts
│   ├── match_metadata/             # Players, teams, historical records
│   └── sample_matches/             # Simulated live match data
├── src/
│   ├── data_ingestion/
│   │   ├── match_data_stream.py    # WebSocket/API listener for live data
│   │   └── player_stats_cache.py   # In-memory player statistics store
│   ├── retrieval/
│   │   ├── vector_store.py         # FAISS/Pinecone wrapper
│   │   └── context_retriever.py    # Queries similar matches & summaries
│   ├── generation/
│   │   ├── prompt_builder.py       # Constructs context-aware prompts
│   │   ├── llm_engine.py           # Fine-tuned model inference
│   │   └── streaming_handler.py    # Token-level streaming logic
│   ├── grounding/
│   │   ├── fact_checker.py         # Validates commentary vs match data
│   │   └── hallucination_filter.py # Blocks impossible claims
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── app.py                  # FastAPI application
│   │   ├── endpoints.py            # HTTP & WebSocket routes
│   │   └── models.py               # Pydantic schemas
│   └── utils/
│       ├── logging_config.py       # Observability setup
│       └── config.py               # Environment & model config
├── notebooks/
│   ├── 01_corpus_analysis.ipynb    # Explore commentary patterns
│   ├── 02_finetuning.ipynb         # Training script walkthrough
│   └── 03_evaluation.ipynb         # Quality metrics
├── tests/
│   ├── test_context_retrieval.py
│   ├── test_fact_checker.py
│   └── test_end_to_end.py
├── docker/
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   └── docker-compose.yml          # Multi-service orchestration
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
"""

class CricketMatchState:
    """Maintains live match state for context injection."""
    def __init__(self, match_id: str, innings_num: int):
        self.match_id = match_id
        self.innings_num = innings_num
        self.runs_scored = 0
        self.wickets_fallen = 0
        self.overs_bowled = 0.0
        self.balls_in_over = 0
        self.batsmen = {"striker": None, "non_striker": None}
        self.bowler = None
        self.run_rate = 0.0
        self.commentary_history = []

    def update_after_delivery(self, runs: int, wicket: bool, bowler_name: str, batsman_name: str):
        """Updates state after each ball bowled."""
        self.runs_scored += runs
        if wicket:
            self.wickets_fallen += 1
        self.balls_in_over += 1
        if self.balls_in_over == 6:
            self.overs_bowled += 1
            self.balls_in_over = 0
        self.batsmen["striker"] = batsman_name
        self.bowler = bowler_name
        self.run_rate = (self.runs_scored / self.overs_bowled) if self.overs_bowled > 0 else 0

    def to_context_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Converts state to prompt context."""
        return {
            "match_id": self.match_id,
            "innings": self.innings_num,
            "runs": self.runs_scored,
            "wickets": self.wickets_fallen,
            "overs": f"{int(self.overs_bowled)}.{self.balls_in_over}",
            "run_rate": round(self.run_rate, 2),
            "striker": self.batsmen["striker"],
            "non_striker": self.batsmen["non_striker"],
            "bowler": self.bowler
        }

class CommentaryGenerationPipeline:
    """Orchestrates entire commentary generation workflow."""
    def __init__(self, model_name: str, vector_store, fact_checker):
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.vector_store = vector_store
        self.fact_checker = fact_checker
        self.prompt_builder = PromptBuilder()
        self.llm_engine = LLMEngine(model_name)

    async def generate_commentary(
        self,
        match_state: CricketMatchState,
        delivery_description: str
    ) -> str:
        """Generates commentary for a single delivery, with streaming."""
        # Step 1: Enrich context via retrieval
        context_dict = match_state.to_context_dict()
        similar_matches = self.vector_store.search(
            query=f"Similar situations: {delivery_description}",
            top_k=3
        )
        context_dict["historical_context"] = similar_matches

        # Step 2: Build prompt
        prompt = self.prompt_builder.build(
            match_state=context_dict,
            delivery=delivery_description,
            style="energetic_broadcast"
        )

        # Step 3: Generate tokens with streaming
        commentary_tokens = []
        async for token in self.llm_engine.stream_generate(prompt, max_tokens=100):
            # Step 4: Real-time fact-checking
            partial_text = "".join(commentary_tokens + [token])
            is_factual = await self.fact_checker.validate(partial_text, context_dict)
            if is_factual:
                commentary_tokens.append(token)
                yield token
            else:
                # Halt generation if hallucination detected
                break

        final_commentary = "".join(commentary_tokens)
        match_state.commentary_history.append(final_commentary)
        return final_commentary

print(project_structure)
print("\n✓ Architecture skeleton defined with cricket-themed components.")
Phase 1 — Core Implementation

Phase 1 establishes the foundation: data ingestion pipeline, base LLM setup, and prompt engineering framework. You will load cricket commentary datasets (from ESPN Cricinfo or YouTube transcripts), tokenize and preprocess them, then implement a basic fine-tuning loop using parameter-efficient methods like LoRA to adapt a pretrained model (e.g., GPT-2 or Llama-2) to cricket-specific language patterns. Simultaneously, build the match state tracker that maintains real-time innings context, runs, wickets, and player statistics. Construct the prompt template that dynamically injects match data into the model's context window, enabling the model to generate commentary conditioned on current game state. Test with simulated match data to verify the full pipeline works end-to-end. This phase does not include RAG retrieval or fact-checking—focus on clean data flow and generating coherent, domain-appropriate text.

🏏 Think of it like cricket: In Phase 1, you're building the broadcasting infrastructure before the match begins. You hire commentators (fine-tune the LLM on cricket commentary datasets), teach them the broadcast style and terminology (LoRA adaptation to cricket patterns), set up the commentary box (prompt template), and prepare the live scoreboard (match state tracker). You don't yet have the analyst team feeding stats in real time, and no fact-checker vetting every claim—you're just ensuring commentators can see the basic match situation (score, overs, batsman names) and generate sensible commentary. Think of the first match commentary as a test broadcast where the commentator speaks based on the scorecard displayed, without real-time interruptions or corrections. This reflects the iterative nature of ML: get the simplest version working first (data → fine-tuning → generation), then add sophistication. Phase 1 is like a full-dress rehearsal with static data, ensuring the machinery operates before live complexity arrives.

# Phase 1: Core Implementation

import json
from typing import List, Dict
import torch
from transformers import GPT2Tokenizer, GPT2LMHeadModel, TextDataset, DataCollatorForLanguageModeling
from transformers import Trainer, TrainingArguments

class CricketCommentaryCorpus:
    """Loads and preprocesses cricket commentary data."""
    def __init__(self, corpus_path: str):
        self.commentary_texts = []
        self.load_corpus(corpus_path)

    def load_corpus(self, path: str):
        """Loads commentary transcripts from file."""
        with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for line in f:
                data = json.loads(line.strip())
                self.commentary_texts.append(data['commentary'])
        print(f"Loaded {len(self.commentary_texts)} commentary samples.")

    def save_to_txt(self, output_path: str):
        """Saves corpus to plain text file for HuggingFace trainer."""
        with open(output_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            for text in self.commentary_texts:
                f.write(text + "\n")

class PromptBuilder:
    """Constructs context-aware prompts for commentary generation."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.template = """
Match Status:
Team 1: {team1}
Team 2: {team2}
Innings: {innings}
Score: {runs}/{wickets} ({overs})
Run Rate: {run_rate}
Strike: {striker}
Bowler: {bowler}

Recent Delivery: {delivery}
Commentary:
"""

    def build(self, match_context: Dict, delivery: str, style: str = "neutral") -> str:
        """Constructs prompt with match context."""
        prompt = self.template.format(
            team1=match_context.get("team1", "India"),
            team2=match_context.get("team2", "Australia"),
            innings=match_context.get("innings", 1),
            runs=match_context.get("runs", 0),
            wickets=match_context.get("wickets", 0),
            overs=match_context.get("overs", "0.0"),
            run_rate=match_context.get("run_rate", 0.0),
            striker=match_context.get("striker", "Virat Kohli"),
            bowler=match_context.get("bowler", "Mitchell Starc"),
            delivery=delivery
        )
        return prompt

class LLMFineTuner:
    """Handles fine-tuning of base LLM on cricket commentary."""
    def __init__(self, model_name: str = "gpt2"):
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name)
        self.model = GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained(model_name)
        self.tokenizer.pad_token = self.tokenizer.eos_token

    def fine_tune(
        self,
        train_file: str,
        output_dir: str = "./models/cricket_gpt2",
        num_epochs: int = 3,
        batch_size: int = 8
    ):
        """Fine-tunes model on cricket commentary corpus."""
        train_dataset = TextDataset(
            tokenizer=self.tokenizer,
            file_path=train_file,
            block_size=128
        )
        data_collator = DataCollatorForLanguageModeling(
            tokenizer=self.tokenizer,
            mlm=False
        )
        training_args = TrainingArguments(
            output_dir=output_dir,
            num_train_epochs=num_epochs,
            per_device_train_batch_size=batch_size,
            save_steps=500,
            save_total_limit=2,
            logging_steps=100,
            weight_decay=0.01,
        )
        trainer = Trainer(
            model=self.model,
            args=training_args,
            data_collator=data_collator,
            train_dataset=train_dataset,
        )
        trainer.train()
        self.model.save_pretrained(output_dir)
        self.tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir)
        print(f"✓ Model fine-tuned and saved to {output_dir}")

    def generate_commentary(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        max_length: int = 100,
        temperature: float = 0.7
    ) -> str:
        """Generates commentary text from prompt."""
        input_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(prompt, return_tensors="pt")
        output_ids = self.model.generate(
            input_ids,
            max_length=max_length,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=0.9,
            do_sample=True,
            pad_token_id=self.tokenizer.eos_token_id
        )
        commentary = self.tokenizer.decode(output_ids[0], skip_special_tokens=True)
        return commentary

# Phase 1 Execution Flow
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # 1. Load and prepare corpus
    print("[Phase 1] Initializing Cricket Commentary System...")
    corpus = CricketCommentaryCorpus("data/commentary_corpus/raw_data.jsonl")
    corpus.save_to_txt("data/commentary_corpus/train.txt")

    # 2. Fine-tune base model
    print("[Phase 1] Fine-tuning LLM on cricket commentary...")
    finetuner = LLMFineTuner(model_name="gpt2")
    finetuner.fine_tune(
        train_file="data/commentary_corpus/train.txt",
        output_dir="models/cricket_gpt2_finetuned",
        num_epochs=3,
        batch_size=8
    )

    # 3. Test with simulated match context
    print("[Phase 1] Testing commentary generation...")
    prompt_builder = PromptBuilder()
    
    match_context = {
        "team1": "India",
        "team2": "Australia",
        "innings": 1,
        "runs": 45,
        "wickets": 2,
        "overs": "5.3",
        "run_rate": 8.45,
        "striker": "Rohit Sharma",
        "bowler": "Jasprit Bumrah"
    }
    delivery_desc = "Short ball, Sharma ducks under it, no run"
    prompt = prompt_builder.build(match_context, delivery_desc)
    
    commentary = finetuner.generate_commentary(prompt, max_length=80)
    print(f"Generated Commentary:\n{commentary}")
    print("\n✓ Phase 1 Complete: Core fine-tuning and generation pipeline established.")
Phase 2 — Feature Completion

Phase 2 introduces retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and fact-checking, transforming the system from standalone generation to grounded, contextually-enriched output. Implement a vector store (using FAISS or a managed service like Pinecone) that indexes historical match summaries, player biographies, career statistics, and notable moments. Build the context retriever that queries this store for matches similar to the current situation, extracting relevant stats and anecdotes to inject into prompts. Implement the fact-checker module that validates generated commentary claims against live match data—verifying that reported runs match the scorecard, wickets are correctly attributed, and player actions align with recorded events. Add streaming endpoints (WebSocket-based) that yield commentary tokens in real time, enabling UI clients to display live commentary as it's generated. Introduce configuration-driven prompt templates to support different commentary styles (energetic, analytical, casual). This phase elevates the system from a basic generator to a production-ready service.

🏏 Think of it like cricket: In Phase 2, you're adding the analyst team, the producer, and the broadcast director to your commentary operation. The analyst room (vector store + retrieval system) continuously feeds relevant context: 'Rohit Sharma has scored 120+ runs in 15 T20s against pace attacks under 130 kmph,' 'This is the 3rd time in 5 overs that the field has been set with 5 fielders on the leg side.' The fact-checker (producer off-mic) validates every claim: 'Wait, the batsman scored 4 runs, not 6—correct that.' The streaming infrastructure (broadcast director) ensures commentary flows to viewers without gaps or delays, sentence by sentence, like a real broadcast. Just as Ravi Shastri's commentary on a Bumrah yorker is informed by Bumrah's economy, the batsman's weakness, and the match situation, your system now combines generation (the commentator's voice) with retrieval (the analyst's insights) and fact-checking (the producer's control). This reveals why grounding matters: without the analyst and fact-checker, a commentator might hallucinate; with them, every word is credible and contextually precise.

# Phase 2: Feature Completion - RAG, Fact-Checking, and Streaming

import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import AsyncIterator
import asyncio
import re
from fastapi import FastAPI, WebSocket
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse

@dataclass
class HistoricalMatch:
    """Historical match record for RAG context."""
    match_id: str
    team1: str
    team2: str
    runs_team1: int
    wickets_team1: int
    overs_bowled: float
    venue: str
    date: str
    summary: str
    player_performances: Dict[str, Dict]  # Player stats

class VectorStore:
    """Simple in-memory vector store for match similarity retrieval (FAISS-like)."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.matches: List[HistoricalMatch] = []
        self.embeddings = np.array([])
        self.load_historical_data()

    def load_historical_data(self):
        """Loads historical cricket matches."""
        sample_matches = [
            HistoricalMatch(
                match_id="IND_vs_AUS_2023_01",
                team1="India",
                team2="Australia",
                runs_team1=185,
                wickets_team1=6,
                overs_bowled=20.0,
                venue="MCG, Melbourne",
                date="2023-12-08",
                summary="India chased 185 with Kohli's 70 and Bumrah's 2/28 bowling.",
                player_performances={
                    "Rohit Sharma": {"runs": 45, "balls": 52, "fours": 3, "sixes": 0},
                    "Virat Kohli": {"runs": 70, "balls": 45, "fours": 8, "sixes": 1},
                    "Jasprit Bumrah": {"wickets": 2, "runs_conceded": 28, "overs": 4.0, "economy": 7.0}
                }
            ),
            HistoricalMatch(
                match_id="IND_vs_AUS_2023_02",
                team1="India",
                team2="Australia",
                runs_team1=165,
                wickets_team1=4,
                overs_bowled=20.0,
                venue="Sydney Cricket Ground",
                date="2023-12-10",
                summary="India defended 165 with strong bowling; Bumrah took 3 wickets.",
                player_performances={
                    "Rohit Sharma": {"runs": 52, "balls": 48, "fours": 4, "sixes": 1},
                    "Jasprit Bumrah": {"wickets": 3, "runs_conceded": 22, "overs": 4.0, "economy": 5.5}
                }
            )
        ]
        self.matches = sample_matches
        # Simulate embeddings (in production, use real embedding model)
        self.embeddings = np.random.randn(len(sample_matches), 768)

    def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 3) -> List[HistoricalMatch]:
        """Retrieves similar historical matches based on query."""
        # Simplified: return random matches; in production, use semantic similarity
        indices = np.random.choice(len(self.matches), min(top_k, len(self.matches)), replace=False)
        return [self.matches[i] for i in indices]

class FactChecker:
    """Validates commentary against match data."""
    def __init__(self, match_state: CricketMatchState):
        self.match_state = match_state

    async def validate(self, text: str, context: Dict) -> bool:
        """Checks if commentary claims are consistent with match state."""
        # Rule 1: Check if reported runs exceed actual runs
        run_claims = re.findall(r'(\d+)\s+runs?|scored\s+(\d+)', text.lower())
        for claim in run_claims:
            claimed_runs = int(claim[0] or claim[1])
            if claimed_runs > context.get("runs", 0):
                print(f"⚠ Hallucination detected: claimed {claimed_runs} runs, actual {context['runs']}")
                return False

        # Rule 2: Check wicket claims
        if "wicket" in text.lower() and context.get("wickets", 0) >= 10:
            print("⚠ Hallucination: all out but commentary mentions more wickets")
            return False

        # Rule 3: Check player names (simple substring match)
        valid_players = [context.get("striker"), context.get("bowler"), "Rohit Sharma", "Jasprit Bumrah"]
        player_mentions = re.findall(r'\b[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][a-z]+\b', text)
        for player in player_mentions:
            if player not in valid_players and "India" not in player:
                print(f"⚠ Warning: mentioned unknown player {player}")
        
        return True

class CommentaryStreamer:
    """Streams commentary tokens in real time."""
    def __init__(self, llm_engine, fact_checker: FactChecker, vector_store: VectorStore):
        self.llm_engine = llm_engine
        self.fact_checker = fact_checker
        self.vector_store = vector_store
        self.prompt_builder = PromptBuilder()

    async def stream_commentary(
        self,
        match_state: CricketMatchState,
        delivery_desc: str,
        style: str = "energetic"
    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Streams commentary tokens with RAG and fact-checking."""
        # Step 1: Retrieve similar matches
        similar_matches = self.vector_store.search(delivery_desc, top_k=2)
        retrieved_context = {
            "similar_venue": similar_matches[0].venue if similar_matches else "N/A",
            "similar_match_summary": similar_matches[0].summary if similar_matches else "N/A"
        }

        # Step 2: Build enriched prompt
        match_dict = match_state.to_context_dict()
        match_dict.update(retrieved_context)
        prompt = self.prompt_builder.build(match_dict, delivery_desc, style)

        # Step 3: Stream generation with fact-checking
        token_buffer = ""
        async for token in self.llm_engine.async_stream_generate(prompt, max_tokens=100):
            token_buffer += token
            
            # Fact-check periodically (every 5 tokens)
            if len(token_buffer.split()) % 5 == 0:
                is_valid = await self.fact_checker.validate(token_buffer, match_dict)
                if not is_valid:
                    print(f"Stopping generation due to hallucination in: {token_buffer}")
                    break
            
            yield token
            await asyncio.sleep(0.01)  # Simulate real-time streaming latency

class LLMEngine:
    """Wrapper for async token generation."""
    def __init__(self, model):
        self.model = model
        self.tokenizer = GPT2Tokenizer.from_pretrained("gpt2")

    async def async_stream_generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        max_tokens: int = 100
    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        """Yields tokens one by one (simulated streaming)."""
        input_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(prompt, return_tensors="pt")
        output_ids = self.model.generate(
            input_ids,
            max_length=max_tokens,
            temperature=0.7,
            output_scores=False,
            return_dict_in_generate=True,
            do_sample=True,
            pad_token_id=self.tokenizer.eos_token_id
        ).sequences
        
        full_text = self.tokenizer.decode(output_ids[0], skip_special_tokens=True)
        for token in full_text.split():
            yield token + " "

# Phase 2 FastAPI Setup
app = FastAPI(title="Cricket Commentary API")
vector_store = VectorStore()

@app.websocket("/ws/commentary/{match_id}")
async def websocket_commentary(websocket: WebSocket, match_id: str):
    """WebSocket endpoint for streaming live commentary."""
    await websocket.accept()
    
    # Simulate match state and delivery
    match_state = CricketMatchState(match_id, innings_num=1)
    match_state.update_after_delivery(runs=4, wicket=False, bowler_name="Jasprit Bumrah", batsman_name="Rohit Sharma")
    
    # Initialize system components
    fact_checker = FactChecker(match_state)
    llm_engine = LLMEngine(GPT2LMHeadModel.from_pretrained("gpt2"))
    streamer = CommentaryStreamer(llm_engine, fact_checker, vector_store)
    
    try:
        while True:
            # Receive delivery description from client
            data = await websocket.receive_json()
            delivery_desc = data.get("delivery", "")
            
            # Stream commentary
            async for token in streamer.stream_commentary(match_state, delivery_desc, style="energetic"):
                await websocket.send_text(token)
            
            # Send completion signal
            await websocket.send_json({"status": "done"})
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"WebSocket error: {e}")
    finally:
        await websocket.close()

@app.get("/health")
def health_check():
    return {"status": "healthy", "system": "Cricket Commentary Generation System"}

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("✓ Phase 2 Complete: RAG retrieval, fact-checking, and streaming enabled.")
    print("  - Vector store with historical matches loaded.")
    print("  - Fact-checker validates commentary against match data.")
    print("  - WebSocket endpoint ready for real-time token streaming.")
    print("  Run with: uvicorn script_name:app --reload")
Phase 3 — Polish & Production Readiness

Phase 3 hardens the system for production: comprehensive error handling, observability logging, unit and integration tests, model quantization for efficient deployment, and containerization. Implement graceful degradation: if the fact-checker blocks commentary due to suspected hallucination, fall back to a templated, data-driven response rather than failing silently. Add structured logging at every stage (data ingestion, retrieval latency, generation time, fact-check results) to monitor system health and debug issues. Write unit tests for the fact-checker, retrieval system, and prompt builder to ensure components work correctly in isolation. Integrate tests validate the full pipeline under realistic match scenarios. Quantize the LLM to 8-bit or 4-bit precision to reduce memory footprint, enabling deployment on cost-effective cloud instances without sacrificing quality. Containerize the service with Docker and orchestrate with Docker Compose or Kubernetes for horizontal scaling. Document the evaluation rubric: measure commentary quality via BLEU score against reference commentary, factual consistency rate (percentage of fact-checked claims that pass), latency (p95 token generation time), and user engagement metrics if deployed to a UI.

# Phase 3: Error Handling, Testing, and Production Deployment

import logging
import json
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch
import bitsandbytes as bnb
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, BitsAndBytesConfig

# ===== LOGGING & OBSERVABILITY =====

class LogLevel(Enum):
    DEBUG = "DEBUG"
    INFO = "INFO"
    WARNING = "WARNING"
    ERROR = "ERROR"

class CommentaryLogger:
    """Structured logging for cricket commentary system."""
    def __init__(self, service_name: str = "cricket_commentary"):
        self.service_name = service_name
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(service_name)
        self.logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
        
        handler = logging.StreamHandler()
        formatter = logging.Formatter(
            '%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s'
        )
        handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        self.logger.addHandler(handler)

    def log_generation_event(
        self,
        match_id: str,
        delivery_num: int,
        prompt_tokens: int,
        generated_tokens: int,
        generation_time_ms: float,
        hallucination_detected: bool
    ):
        """Logs generation metrics for observability."""
        event = {
            "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "event_type": "commentary_generated",
            "match_id": match_id,
            "delivery_num": delivery_num,
            "prompt_tokens": prompt_tokens,
            "generated_tokens": generated_tokens,
            "generation_time_ms": generation_time_ms,
            "hallucination_detected": hallucination_detected,
            "tokens_per_second": generated_tokens / (generation_time_ms / 1000)
        }
        self.logger.info(json.dumps(event))

    def log_error(
        self,
        match_id: str,
        error_type: str,
        error_message: str,
        stack_trace: str = ""
    ):
        """Logs errors with full context."""
        error_event = {
            "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            "event_type": "error",
            "match_id": match_id,
            "error_type": error_type,
            "error_message": error_message,
            "stack_trace": stack_trace
        }
        self.logger.error(json.dumps(error_event))

# ===== ERROR HANDLING & FALLBACKS =====

class CommentaryGenerationError(Exception):
    """Base exception for commentary generation failures."""
    pass

class HallucinationDetectedError(CommentaryGenerationError):
    """Raised when fact-checker blocks hallucinated output."""
    pass

class TemplateFallbackCommentary:
    """Generates data-driven commentary when LLM fails."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.templates = {
            "boundary": "{batsman} hits a {runs}-run boundary! {team} score now {total_runs}.",
            "wicket": "WICKET! {batsman} has been dismissed by {bowler}. {team} {runs}/{wickets}.",
            "dot": "{bowler} bowls a dot ball. No runs. {team} {runs}/{wickets}.",
            "maiden_over": "A maiden over from {bowler}! No runs in the over."
        }

    def generate(self, event_type: str, context: Dict) -> str:
        """Generates template-based commentary."""
        if event_type not in self.templates:
            return f"Ball {context.get('ball_num')}: {context.get('runs')} runs, {context.get('wicket')} wickets."
        template = self.templates[event_type]
        try:
            return template.format(**context)
        except KeyError:
            return f"Generic update: {context.get('description', 'Play continues.')}"

class RobustCommentaryPipeline:
    """Wrapper with error handling and fallback mechanisms."""
    def __init__(self, core_pipeline, logger: CommentaryLogger):
        self.core_pipeline = core_pipeline
        self.logger = logger
        self.fallback_generator = TemplateFallbackCommentary()

    async def generate_with_fallback(
        self,
        match_state: CricketMatchState,
        delivery_desc: str,
        match_id: str
    ) -> str:
        """Generates commentary with graceful degradation."""
        try:
            # Attempt primary LLM-based generation
            import time
            start_time = time.time()
            
            commentary = await self.core_pipeline.generate_commentary(match_state, delivery_desc)
            
            generation_time_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
            self.logger.log_generation_event(
                match_id=match_id,
                delivery_num=int(match_state.overs_bowled * 6 + match_state.balls_in_over),
                prompt_tokens=len(delivery_desc.split()),
                generated_tokens=len(commentary.split()),
                generation_time_ms=generation_time_ms,
                hallucination_detected=False
            )
            return commentary

        except HallucinationDetectedError as e:
            # Fallback to template-based generation
            self.logger.log_error(
                match_id=match_id,
                error_type="HallucinationDetected",
                error_message=str(e)
            )
            fallback_context = {
                "batsman": match_state.batsmen["striker"],
                "bowler": match_state.bowler,
                "team": "India",
                "total_runs": match_state.runs_scored,
                "runs": match_state.runs_scored,
                "wickets": match_state.wickets_fallen,
                "runs_in_ball": 4,
                "ball_num": int(match_state.overs_bowled * 6 + match_state.balls_in_over)
            }
            return self.fallback_generator.generate("boundary", fallback_context)

        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.log_error(
                match_id=match_id,
                error_type=type(e).__name__,
                error_message=str(e)
            )
            return f"Commentary unavailable for delivery {int(match_state.overs_bowled)}.{match_state.balls_in_over}. Score: {match_state.runs_scored}/{match_state.wickets_fallen}."

# ===== MODEL QUANTIZATION FOR EFFICIENT DEPLOYMENT =====

class QuantizedLLMEngine:
    """Loads and runs 8-bit quantized LLM for cost-effective inference."""
    def __init__(self, model_name: str = "meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-hf"):
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.quantization_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
            load_in_8bit=True,
            llm_int8_enable_fp32_cpu_offload=False,
            llm_int8_threshold=6.0
        )
        self.load_quantized_model()

    def load_quantized_model(self):
        """Loads model with 8-bit quantization."""
        print(f"Loading {self.model_name} with 8-bit quantization...")
        self.model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
            self.model_name,
            quantization_config=self.quantization_config,
            device_map="auto"
        )
        self.tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.model_name)
        print(f"✓ Model loaded. Memory footprint reduced by ~4x via 8-bit quantization.")

    def generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        max_tokens: int = 100,
        temperature: float = 0.7
    ) -> str:
        """Generates text from quantized model."""
        input_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(prompt, return_tensors="pt").to(self.model.device)
        output_ids = self.model.generate(
            input_ids,
            max_length=max_tokens,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=0.9,
            do_sample=True
        )
        return self.tokenizer.decode(output_ids[0], skip_special_tokens=True)

# ===== UNIT & INTEGRATION TESTS =====

class TestFactChecker:
    """Unit tests for fact-checking module."""
    @pytest.fixture
    def fact_checker(self):
        match_state = CricketMatchState("TEST_001", innings_num=1)
        match_state.update_after_delivery(runs=45, wicket=False, bowler_name="Bumrah", batsman_name="Sharma")
        return FactChecker(match_state)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_valid_commentary(self, fact_checker):
        """Tests that valid commentary passes fact-check."""
        context = {"runs": 45, "wickets": 2, "striker": "Rohit Sharma", "bowler": "Jasprit Bumrah"}
        result = await fact_checker.validate("Sharma scores 4 runs.", context)
        assert result is True

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_hallucination_detection(self, fact_checker):
        """Tests hallucination detection when claimed runs exceed actual."""
        context = {"runs": 20, "wickets": 1, "striker": "Rohit Sharma", "bowler": "Jasprit Bumrah"}
        result = await fact_checker.validate("Sharma has now scored 100 runs!", context)
        assert result is False

class TestPromptBuilder:
    """Unit tests for prompt construction."""
    def test_prompt_includes_all_fields(self):
        builder = PromptBuilder()
        context = {
            "team1": "India",
            "team2": "Australia",
            "innings": 1,
            "runs": 85,
            "wickets": 3,
            "overs": "10.2",
            "run_rate": 8.3,
            "striker": "Virat Kohli",
            "bowler": "Mitchell Starc"
        }
        prompt = builder.build(context, "Kohli plays a drive to cover")
        assert "India" in prompt
        assert "85" in prompt
        assert "Virat Kohli" in prompt
        assert "Mitchell Starc" in prompt
        assert "10.2" in prompt

class TestEndToEnd:
    """Integration tests for full pipeline."""
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_match_to_commentary_flow(self):
        """Tests data flow from match state to generated commentary."""
        # Create match state
        match_state = CricketMatchState("IND_vs_AUS_2024", innings_num=1)
        match_state.update_after_delivery(runs=4, wicket=False, bowler_name="Jasprit Bumrah", batsman_name="Rohit Sharma")
        match_state.update_after_delivery(runs=0, wicket=False, bowler_name="Jasprit Bumrah", batsman_name="Rohit Sharma")
        
        # Verify state
        assert match_state.runs_scored == 4
        assert match_state.balls_in_over == 2
        assert match_state.run_rate == (4 / (2 / 6))  # ~12
        
        # Verify context dict
        context = match_state.to_context_dict()
        assert context["runs"] == 4
        assert context["overs"] == "0.2"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Initialize logging
    logger = CommentaryLogger("production_system")
    logger.logger.info("Starting Phase 3: Production Hardening")
    
    # Run unit tests
    print("\n[Phase 3] Running unit tests...")
    pytest.main(["-v", __file__, "-k", "Test"])
    
    # Test quantization
    print("\n[Phase 3] Testing quantized model loading...")
    # Uncomment with valid HuggingFace credentials

Evaluation Rubric

items: Factual Consistency (30%): Percentage of generated commentary claims that pass fact-checking against live match data; no hallucinated runs, wickets, or player actions.
Commentary Fluency & Coherence (25%): BLEU score vs. reference professional commentary; evaluates grammatical quality, terminology accuracy, and narrative flow.
Contextual Relevance (20%): Retrieved historical matches and stats meaningfully enhance generated commentary; RAG augmentation measurably improves output quality.
System Latency & Throughput (15%): p95 token generation latency < 500ms; streaming endpoints serve >10 concurrent match feeds without degradation.
Error Handling & Resilience (10%): System gracefully degrades to template-based commentary when LLM fails; no service crashes under load or invalid input.

sections.content: Extension Challenges: (1) Multi-language commentary: extend system to generate commentary in Hindi, Bengali, Tamil alongside English by fine-tuning on multilingual corpora. (2) Real-time statistics aggregation: integrate with live cricket APIs (ESPN Cricinfo, Sportradar) for automatic match data ingestion instead of simulated streams. (3) Player sentiment modeling: predict and inject emotional context ('This is a must-win situation for Bumrah') by analyzing match pressure, team ranking, and tournament stage. (4) Commentary style transfer: allow commentators to specify style ('energetic like Harsha Bhogle' vs. 'analytical like Michael Holding') via prompt-based style tokens. (5) User feedback loop: collect fan engagement metrics and user ratings of commentary to retrain the model on high-engagement examples, improving quality over time. (6) Multimodal integration: incorporate video frames to detect player celebrations, field placements, and ball trajectories, enhancing commentary with visual context. (7) Tournament context: track multi-match tournament narratives (e.g., 'India needs a win to qualify; this match decides their fate') and weave them into match-level commentary.
